description_long: This object is a large, cubic chessboard with a classic black-and-white checkered pattern covering all visible faces. The squares are arranged in a regular grid, with alternating light and dark tiles that create a visually striking, geometric appearance. The board appears to be constructed from a hard, solid material, likely wood or plastic, with a smooth, matte finish. The edges are sharp and clean, with no visible ornamentation or inscriptions along the border. The chessboard is oversized compared to standard boards, suggesting it may serve as a decorative or functional urban feature, such as a public game table or a sculptural element in a park. No chess pieces or additional components are present on the surface.
description: A large, cubic chessboard with a classic black-and-white checkered pattern and smooth, clean edges. The board is oversized and features a regular grid of alternating squares.
description_view_0: The front face displays a full grid of alternating black and white squares, with sharp, clean edges and no visible border details.
description_view_1: This side view is nearly identical to the front, showing the same checkered pattern and uniform surface.
description_view_2: The opposite face again features the regular checkered grid, with no distinguishing marks or features.
description_view_3: This view mirrors the others, maintaining the consistent black-and-white checkered pattern and smooth finish.
attribute_board_pattern: The board features a regular, alternating pattern of black and white squares arranged in a perfect grid, with each square being of equal size and shape. The pattern is uninterrupted and extends to the edges of each visible face, with no decorative or unique elements beyond the classic checkered design.
attribute_border_design: The border is minimal and unadorned, with the checkered pattern extending to the very edge of the board. There is no visible color differentiation, ornamentation, or inscriptions along the border; it is defined only by the sharp, clean termination of the checkered surface.
